Patch Set 1:

So I got it to build and work on Ubuntu17 but now it appears to not build on Ubuntu16. I don't have a Ubuntu16 system anymore so could someone look into why this doesn't work while at the same time making sure Ubuntu17 still works.

Patch Set 1:

James, I tried to build it on Debian 9 - 4.9.0-4-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.9.51-1 (2017-09-28) x86_64 GNU/Linux and run into this problem:

libtool: compile:  gcc -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I. -I.. -include /home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/undef.h -include /home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/config.h -I/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/libcfs/include -I/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/lnet/include -I/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/lnet/include/uapi -I/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/lustre/include -I/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/lustre/include/uapi -DNETSNMP_ENABLE_IPV6 -fno-strict-aliasing -g -O2 -fdebug-prefix-map=/build/net-snmp-fJjPtm/net-snmp-5.7.3+dfsg=. -fstack-protector-strong -Wformat -Werror=format-security -DNETSNMP_USE_INLINE -Ulinux -Dlinux=linux -D_REENTRANT -D_GNU_SOURCE -DDEBIAN -fwrapv -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe -I/usr/local/include -D_LARGEFILE_SOURCE -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -I/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/perl/5.24/CORE -Wdate-time -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -I/usr/include -g -O2 -Wall -Werror -MT liblustresnmp_la-lustre-snmp-util.lo -MD -MP -MF .deps/liblustresnmp_la-lustre-snmp-util.Tpo -c lustre-snmp-util.c -o liblustresnmp_la-lustre-snmp-util.o >/dev/null 2>&1
mv -f .deps/liblustresnmp_la-lustre-snmp-util.Tpo .deps/liblustresnmp_la-lustre-snmp-util.Plo
make[3]: Leaving directory '/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/snmp'
Makefile:632: recipe for target 'all-recursive' failed
make[2]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
make[2]: Leaving directory '/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541/snmp'
autoMakefile:599: recipe for target 'all-recursive' failed
make[1]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/tstibor/lustre-release-lu-5541'
autoMakefile:487: recipe for target 'all' failed
make: *** [all] Error 2

It was configured as follows: ./configure --disable-server 
and libsnmp* is installed, so it tried to build with snmp support.

>dpkg --list | grep snmp
ii  libsnmp-base                     5.7.3+dfsg-1.7                 all          SNMP configuration script, MIBs and documentation
ii  libsnmp-dev                      5.7.3+dfsg-1.7                 amd64        SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) development files
ii  libsnmp30:amd64                  5.7.3+dfsg-1.7                 amd64        SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) library

I guess the Problem is in "lustre-release-lu-5541/snmp/Makefile.am"

Patch Set 1:

Removing these lines as also done in the other Makefile.am files compiles with snmp on Debian 9

diff --git a/snmp/Makefile.am b/snmp/Makefile.am
index 453f3fffc7..f616215f65 100644
--- a/snmp/Makefile.am
+++ b/snmp/Makefile.am
@@ -9,10 +9,8 @@ liblustresnmp_la_SOURCES =     \
        lustre-snmp-util.c      \
        lustre-snmp-util.h
 
-LIBCFS = $(top_builddir)/libcfs/libcfs/libcfs.a
-
 liblustresnmp_la_CFLAGS := $(NET_SNMP_CFLAGS)
-liblustresnmp_la_LIBADD := $(NET_SNMP_LIBS) $(LIBCFS)
+liblustresnmp_la_LIBADD := $(NET_SNMP_LIBS)
 liblustresnmp_la_LDFLAGS := -version-info 1:0:0
 
 SUBDIRS =

The problem is triggered by this configure combination:

./configure --disable-server  --disable-quilt --disable-dependency-tracking --disable-doc --disable-utils --disable-iokit --disable-snmp --disable-tests --enable-quota --with-kmp-moddir=updates

parents.subject: LU-8358 vvp: Print discarded page warning on -EIO

commit.subject: LU-5541 build: move libcfs and liblustreapi over to libtool
commit.message:
LU-5541 build: move libcfs and liblustreapi over to libtool

Change libcfs into a convenience library using libtool. This allows
use to embbed libcfs library into both liblnetconfig and liblustreapi
so their is no longer a need to link applications to libcfs.a
anymore. With this change we need migrate liblustreapi to libtool.

libtool knows how to build both static and dymanic libraries for
liblusteapi, so no need to hack the Makefile. As two added benefits,
the utilities will now use the dynamic version, thus reducing their
footprint, and calling make twice in a row won't rebuild objects
already built.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: Icc1e5d42df503b9bf393396fe09f4e4f1f242486
Signed-off-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30562
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
